generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT device for real-time occupancy detection integrated into the lighting infrastructure of the LEED Platinum academic building"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Multi-parameter sensor measuring CO2 levels, temperature, humidity, and air quality within the indoor environment"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

instance LightLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring real-time light levels for occupancy-based lighting control optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightLevelDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyMonitoringSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Device tracking energy consumption metrics for building control systems and performance optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
]

instance LightLevelDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightLevelSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyMonitoringSensor
]

instance EnrichedBIMDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor, LightLevelSensor, OccupancySensor
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BIM model data incorporating real-time operational parameters for dynamic twin representation"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Foundational Building Information Modeling (BIM) artifact containing geometric, spatial, and functional characteristics of building components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto, UnrealEngine
]

instance OperationalDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Centralized repository for real-time operational data including sensor telemetry, environmental parameters, and facility management logs"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalDataTransmitted, LightLevelDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, EnrichedBIMDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance SimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model-driven simulation engine for validating lighting control semantics and energy efficiency strategies using Unreal Engine physics simulations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngine
]

instance ControlSemanticsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Rule-based control model for occupancy-aware lighting and environmental control optimization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "IoT connectivity framework providing secure, scalable device management with MQTT-based communication for physical sensor integration"
    DTDFVocab:enables IoTDataIngestionService, DeviceManagementService
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin enabler for asset modeling and real-time data synchronization serving as the central data hub for the system"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlSemanticsValidationService, DataIntegrationService
]

instance UnrealEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-performance 3D rendering engine enabling real-time interactive visualization and physics simulations of the digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables ImmersiveVisualizationService, ScenarioSimulationService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework implementing MDE principles for rule-based control model validation and parameterized simulations"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlSemanticsValidationService
]

// C6: Services
instance IoTDataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for secure, low-latency ingestion of IoT sensor data from physical building systems into the digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeDataStreamInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing continuous real-time monitoring of building systems with event-driven data streams for occupancy detection and energy tracking"
    DTDFVocab:provides OccupancyAwarenessInsight, EnergyTrackingInsight
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service offering 3D interactive visualization of building systems through Unreal Engine for navigation, inspection, and system interaction"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInterfaceAction
]

instance ControlSemanticsValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating control semantics through rule-based and AI-driven models to optimize energy use and occupant comfort"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlOptimizationInsight
]

instance ScenarioSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling what-if scenario testing for lighting schedules, occupancy patterns, and energy efficiency strategies"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceInsight
]

instance DeviceManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing IoT device configuration and runtime adjustments without redeployment"
    DTDFVocab:provides ConfigurationManagementAction
]

instance DataIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service harmonizing data from disparate sources (IoT devices, legacy systems) into unified digital twin representations"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataConsistencyInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeDataStreamInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing continuous event-driven data streams from physical sensors to the digital twin"
]

instance OccupancyAwarenessInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight enabling occupancy-based building control optimization through real-time occupancy detection"
]

instance EnergyTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight monitoring and tracking energy consumption metrics for performance optimization"
]

instance ControlOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing recommendations for optimal control strategies based on rule-based and AI-driven models"
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight enabling predictive maintenance scenarios through simulation-based analysis"
]

instance DataConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and completeness across integrated systems"
]

instance VisualizationInterfaceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action enabling immersive 3D navigation and interaction with the digital twin through Unreal Engine"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ConfigurationManagementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action allowing runtime configuration adjustments to data collection parameters without system redeployment"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environments/Components
instance AcademicBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "LEED Platinum certified academic building serving as the physical twin for the digital twin system"
    base:contains LightingInfrastructure, EnvironmentalMonitoringSystem, OccupancyControlSystem
]

instance LightingInfrastructure : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Building control system components including sensors, actuators, and control nodes for real-time lighting management"
    base:isContainedIn AcademicBuildingEnvironment
]

instance EnvironmentalMonitoringSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Integrated system of CO2, thermal, and air quality sensors providing environmental parameter monitoring"
    base:isContainedIn AcademicBuildingEnvironment
]

instance OccupancyControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "System enabling occupancy-aware control of building systems through real-time data processing"
    base:isContainedIn AcademicBuildingEnvironment
]

// C12: Twinning Process Components
instance DeviceModelingTemplate : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MDE-driven template for defining Building Control System components within BIM for semantic bridge creation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance ModelTransformationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated engine transforming enriched BIM models into deployable digital twin artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:enables ArtifactGenerationService
]

instance ArtifactGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service automating the generation of deployable digital twin components from BIM models"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeployableArtifactInsight
]

instance DeployableArtifactInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight confirming successful generation of deployable digital twin components with automated validation"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system enables **facility managers** to exert control over physical infrastructure via the digital twin, facilitated by **bidirectional communication** between the DT and physical assets. The framework supports **command execution** for operational adjustments (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting control) but does not specify **control message formats** (e.g., JSON, XML) or **validation/authentication mechanisms**. **Feedback systems** are implied through the real-time synchronization loop, though **conflict resolution** or **command execution confirmation** protocols are not detailed. The system’s **modular architecture** and **runtime configuration management** allow dynamic adjustments to control logic without redeployment, ensuring adaptability to operational changes. Interaction roles (e.g., *Facility Managers*) are defined to manage physical control via the DT interface."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system supports **real-time data exchange capabilities**, ensuring continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts with **low-latency requirements** to maintain operational relevance. The framework emphasizes **dynamic operational needs** over static BIM representations, enabling **high-frequency updates** (implied by 'continuous synchronization') for monitoring and control. Runtime configuration management allows adjustments without redeployment, suggesting adaptability to varying temporal granularity demands (e.g., millisecond-level for control systems or second-level for analytics). Latency constraints are not explicitly quantified, but the emphasis on real-time implies sub-second synchronization intervals for critical operational services (e.g., HVAC, lighting)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **centralized hub-and-spoke architecture** with **coordinated roles and system components** (as depicted in Figure 1). The framework acts as a **central hub** enabling bidirectional data exchange between enriched BIM models and operational twins, orchestrating interactions among **Building Control Engineers**, **Facility Managers**, and **DT Engineers**. Resource management is implied through **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. Component integration is supported via **standardized extension points**, ensuring modularity and adaptability. System-wide coordination relies on **bidirectional synchronization** and **service-oriented design**, enabling holistic building lifecycle management with **low-overhead updates** for maintainability."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) system described in the provided documents supports a **model-driven life cycle** spanning **device modeling, deployment, runtime synchronization, and operational extension**, addressing BIM’s limitations in dynamic representation. The lifecycle begins with **Building Control Engineers** defining **sensors, actuators, and controllers** within enriched BIM models, creating a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase leverages **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** principles to extract structured representations from BIM, enabling automated generation of deployable DT artifacts with minimal manual intervention.

Transitioning to **runtime**, the framework ensures **real-time data exchange** between physical infrastructure and the DT, synchronized via standardized interfaces. **Facility Managers** interact with the twin for monitoring and control, while **DT Engineers** extend functionality through **custom services and plugins**, integrated via modular architecture with **standardized extension points**. The lifecycle governance emphasizes **continuous synchronization** and **immersive visualization** (e.g., Unreal Engine) for operational oversight. However, explicit phases like **retirement or legacy migration** are not detailed in the documents.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system addresses fidelity and validity through **real-time data synchronization** using **Eclipse Hono** and **Eclipse Ditto**, which minimize discrepancies between physical assets and their digital representations by enabling live updates. Validation is implicitly supported by the **semantic coherence** of BIM-based models, ensuring consistency between design-time and runtime data. However, explicit **fidelity metrics** (e.g., quantitative error quantification or uncertainty assessments) are not detailed in the provided documents. The system does not explicitly mention **error detection mechanisms**, **uncertainty quantification**, or **quality assurance processes** beyond the foundational reliance on **policy and regulatory mandates** for BIM adoption, which indirectly support data integrity. The **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** (referenced in abstracts or related work) is not described in the provided context documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **DT hosting and deployment** framework is designed around a **model-driven approach**, emphasizing **scalability** (NF_X) and **maintainability** through low-overhead updates. While specific **hosting platforms** (cloud/edge/on-premise) are not mentioned, the system supports **scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems** via its **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points**. **Deployment models** are implied to align with operational needs, with a focus on **runtime configuration management** to enable adjustments without full redeployment. **Computational and storage requirements** are not specified, but the framework prioritizes **accessibility for users with varying technical expertise** (NF_X), ensuring usability across roles (e.g., *Building Control Engineers*, *Facility Managers*). **Availability/reliability assurance** is addressed indirectly through interoperability and modularity, though explicit redundancy or failover mechanisms are not detailed."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** through non-functional requirements (NF_X) addressing cross-platform compatibility and standardized extension points. It supports **scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems**, enabling integration with external tools and enterprise systems via unspecified protocols. The framework does not explicitly mention specific data exchange standards (e.g., IFC, OPC UA) or dependencies on external systems beyond the implied need for **bidirectional data flow** with BIM tools (e.g., Revit) and operational systems. Horizontal integration is framed as a **requirements-driven approach** to bridge static BIM representations and dynamic operational needs, with a focus on low-overhead updates and accessibility."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The framework adheres to **BIM standards** as its foundational data model but does not explicitly reference **IFC 4.0**, **gbXML**, or **OPC UA v1.04**. **Connectivity standards** are implied through mentions of **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for IoT integration, but protocols like **CoAP** or **STEP-AP 242** are not detailed. **Security compliance** is not addressed beyond general mentions of **DT deployment frameworks**, and frameworks like **ISO 27001** or **FIPS 140-2** are not referenced. The system emphasizes **interoperability** via modular design but lacks explicit standardization profiles beyond BIM and IoT connectivity.
"
]
